title: Racosperma retrorsum
taxonomy: Racosperma retrorsum is classified within the family Fabaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as legumes. It is also classified as a member of the subfamily Mimosoideae, which is a group of plants within the Fabaceae family. The plant is native to Australia and has been reclassified from the genus Acacia to Racosperma. Racosperma retrorsum is a shrub or small tree with a distinctive appearance, including its sharp prickles. The classification of Racosperma retrorsum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.
uses: Racosperma retrorsum, also known as the prickly acacia, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and pods have been used to treat ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
